title: radiomics-phantom: A Physics-Based Stability Atlas for IBSI Radiomics Features Using Synthetic Digital Phantoms
description:
An open, fully synthetic research kernel (IORN-002) for mapping the stability of radiomics features under acquisition variation. It provides deterministic digital texture phantoms with known ground truth, a from-scratch feature core reproducing all 482 IBSI-1 digital-phantom reference values, an acquisition simulator, an ICC/CCC feature-stability atlas, and physics-based feature normalisation.

This is a methodological software contribution using synthetic digital phantoms only: it involves no human subjects, no patient data, no DICOM, and no deep learning, and makes no clinical performance claims. Every volume is generated from a seed and every feature is computed from first principles using numpy/scipy/scikit-image only.

Code is released under the MIT License; the accompanying prose and figures are released under CC BY 4.0.
